title: Lycopodioides perpusilla
taxonomy: Lycopodioides perpusilla is a species of lycopod, a group of ancient and primitive vascular plants. It belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae, which is a family of club mosses. The genus Lycopodioides is a small group of lycopods, and L. perpusilla is one of the species within this genus. This classification places L. perpusilla in the division Lycopodiophyta, which is a division of vascular plants that are thought to have evolved over 400 million years ago. The classification of L. perpusilla reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Lycopodioides perpusilla, also known as club moss or spike moss, is a type of small, evergreen, vascular plant that belongs to the club moss family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Lycopodioides perpusilla include lignans, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and hepatoprotective properties.
